Table of Contents

Part 2

Navigating Folders

Section

Navigating Folders

What is navigation?

When you look in folders, and move from one folder to another, it is known as navigating. If your job or study involves computers, you might sometimes be asked to navigate (or go) to a folder. This involves moving to the folder in question.

Folder paths

When you have to more than one folder to get where you need to be, it is known as a folder path. A folder path is a list of folder names that finish at a particular folder.

So, if you have a folder called Photos, and that folder is in the folder Documents, the path to the Photos folder is Documents → Photos.

Don't worry if you don't understand folder paths straight away, you'll do some exercises that will help explain them.

In the next activity, you are going to be identifying folders within a folder structure. Click the link below to move on.
